items.title: Just tried @RefineInk, and it is the most granular feedback I have ever received on a paper. Along with dozens of small and big errors/issues, it caug...
items.description_html: Just tried @RefineInk, and it is the most granular feedback I have ever received on a paper. Along with dozens of small and big errors/issues, it caught one sentence where I slightly understate the claim of another scholar in the literature review. I was so sure I was right and went back and read the paper, but @RefineInk was right, I did understate their claim. This will become a part of redrafting all my future papers. Big shoutout to @ben_golub and his team.

items.title: RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni: I have known @deepakshenoy on Twitter for a long time, and I’ve always enjoyed reading his views and though...
items.description_html:
RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni
I have known @deepakshenoy on Twitter for a long time, and I’ve always enjoyed reading his views and thoughts on finance, investing, savings, the stock market, and all of that. He’s a great mind that I truly admire.

I had heard him speak in Kannada on a podcast called Taleharate a few years ago, run by a dear friend, @zeusisdead, and I knew that he could comfortably converse in Kannada. When we reached out with a request to come on @mundhebanni, he immediately said yes, showed up on time, and we had a fascinating discussion.

I also learned a great deal about what it really takes when someone is deeply passionate about something and wants to build a career around it. Deepak is an NITK Surathkal graduate and spent at least the first 10 years of his career building in tech, before pivoting into something he was truly passionate about, finance. He started out with a blog, and that blog’s success eventually led to the creation of one of India’s most respected investment research and portfolio management companies, Capitalmind, managing thousands of crores.

In a signature Mundhe Banni style, this story is a mix of three things: his personal journey, professional success, and his advice and perspective on personal finance.

I also learned that he was in Mumbai on the day of 26/11 and had narrowly escaped the tragedy!

The full episode is dropping this evening.

Watch the trailer 👇

Subscribe to @mundhebanni YT to get notified:
https://www.youtube.com/@mundhebanni

@akaranth @Shishir_S_U @kodlady

items.title: The threat to any negotiations isn't Iran or the US, apparently. There is one nation that abhors the lack of violence, and strategically will scuttle ...
items.description_html:
The threat to any negotiations isn't Iran or the US, apparently. There is one nation that abhors the lack of violence, and strategically will scuttle negotiations or an end to the war.

There is no point relying on statements made by heads of state anymore. In a war, information is a weapon, and misinformation is crucial. The war does not end with information. It ends when hostilities stop. Relying on statements, and markets dancing to the tunes of a tweet in capslock is basically like we haven't learnt a THING from the last few months.

The only thing you can learn from what someone says about a war, especially if he's a party that's in the war, is that you can't learn anything from it. Don't rejoice in advance.

items.title: China's industrial sector is under heavy pressure: 23.8% of Chinese industrial firms were unprofitable in 2025, the highest since 2000 and doubling si...
items.description_html:
China's industrial sector is under heavy pressure:

23.8% of Chinese industrial firms were unprofitable in 2025, the highest since 2000 and doubling since 2017.

This marks the 4th consecutive annual increase, their worst streak on record.

Profit margins for private industrial firms are down to just 4.5%, the lowest since at least 2014 and down for 4 straight years.

This comes as China continues to battle the longest deflation streak on record, weak domestic demand, and now uncertainty about energy costs due to the Iran War.

China's industrial recovery remains nowhere in sight.

items.title: BREAKING: Foreign holdings of US Treasuries surged +$34.8 billion in January, to $9.3 trillion, the 2nd-highest on record. Japan, the largest foreign-...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Foreign holdings of US Treasuries surged +$34.8 billion in January, to $9.3 trillion, the 2nd-highest on record.

Japan, the largest foreign-owner, purchased +$39.8 billion, bringing the total to $1.2 trillion, the highest since July 2022.

The UK, the 2nd-largest holder, posted a +$29.3 billion increase, to $895.3 billion, the 3rd-highest on record.

China's stockpile, the 3rd-largest holder, increased +$10.9 billion, to $694.4 billion, the highest since September.

Meanwhile, European Union holdings rose +$8.0 billion, to $2.1 trillion.

Foreigners now own more US debt than ever.

items.title: Fertilizer prices are skyrocketing: New Orleans granular urea prices, the most widely used nitrogen fertilizer, are up +89% since December, to $660 pe...
items.description_html:
Fertilizer prices are skyrocketing:

New Orleans granular urea prices, the most widely used nitrogen fertilizer, are up +89% since December, to $660 per short ton, the highest since September 2022.

By comparison, the 2022 peak during the Russia-Ukraine war was $910 per short ton.

This comes as the Strait of Hormuz shutdown has effectively halted fertilizer shipments from the Middle East.

Furthermore, top exporters China and Russia are also curbing crop nutrient sales, tightening supply further at the worst possible time, right before the spring planting season.

Higher fertilizer costs directly translate to higher food prices, threatening to reignite agricultural inflation as seen in 2022.

Food is set to get even more expensive.

items.title: Intraday market volatility has rarely been this elevated: Front-month $VIX futures intra-day volatility is up to 388.2, the highest reading in at leas...
items.description_html:
Intraday market volatility has rarely been this elevated:

Front-month $VIX futures intra-day volatility is up to 388.2, the highest reading in at least 6 months.

This index tracks $VIX futures price swings within a single trading day, measured in 15-minute intervals.

The current reading is ~4 TIMES higher than the average level, usually associated with full-blown market panic.

Meanwhile, S&P 500 implied volatility, showing how much the index is expected to move in a day, is up to ~1.77%, the highest since April 2025.

Despite this, the number of sessions with moves greater than 1.75% over the last 3 months is just 2.

Put simply, futures and options markets are pricing in far more volatility than the S&P 500 is actually realizing.

Uncertainty is at unprecedented levels.

items.description_html:
The market is experiencing historically high intraday volatility:

The number of intraday reversals in the S&P 500 over the last 3 months is up to 28, the highest since 2015.

This means that in nearly half of all trading sessions over the last 3 months, the market opened either higher or lower than the previous day and erased that opening move.

This is also the 2nd-highest reading since the 2008 Financial Crisis, which peaked at 35 such days.

Headline-induced volatility is extremely high.

BREAKING: Iran says it could shut the vital Bab el-Mandeb Strait if attacks are carried out on its territory or islands.

Details include:

1. The Bab al-Mandeb Strait is a narrow passage linking the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden and the Arabian Sea

2. ~12% of global seaborne oil passes through Bab al-Mandab

3. The Bab al-Mandeb Strait is the world’s 4th-largest shipping chokepoint

4. If both Hormuz and Mandeb are closed, total offline capacity could near 25 million barrels per day, or ~25% of global supply

We now await the US' response to Iran's "five conditions" for a ceasefire.

Iran -> Non-hostile ships can transit Strait of Hormuz

1. "Non-hostile vessels... benefit from safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz in coordination with the competent Iranian authorities"

2. "Vessels, equipment & any assets belonging to the aggressor parties - namely U.S. & the Israeli regime... do not qualify for innocent or non-hostile passage"

3. "Responsibility for any disruption, insecurity, or escalation of risks in this critical waterway & its surrounding region rests squarely with the U.S. & Israeli regime"

4. "The full restoration of security & sustainable stability in the Strait is contingent upon the cessation of military aggression & threats... and full respect for Iran’s legitimate rights & interests"

5. "The Strait of Hormuz remains open & maritime traffic has not been suspended. Navigation continues, subject to compliance with the necessary measures referenced above"

• ~30GW annual additions expected over medium term

• Strong play in tubes for rooftop, ground-mounted & tracker structures

• Rapid industrial expansion driving hollow section demand
• APL Apollo holds 60–70% market share in this segment
• Management expects 20–25% growth for next 3–4 years in PEB-related demand

Solar + PEB together significantly expand the structural steel tubes TAM — positioning APL Apollo for sustained volume growth.

items.title: Viviana Power Tech is gradually moving beyond pure EPC into a mix of execution and annuity-based visibility. Order book stands at ~₹1,400 Cr. FY26 re...
items.description_html:
Viviana Power Tech is gradually moving beyond pure EPC into a mix of execution and annuity-based visibility.

Order book stands at ~₹1,400 Cr.
FY26 revenue guidance is ₹560 Cr (vs ₹219 Cr last year).
Management is guiding 40–50% CAGR over the next 2–3 years.

The company has entered BESS with ~₹500 Cr worth of secured projects (100 MW Rajasthan, 65 MW Gujarat).
~₹300 Cr capex, funded 80% debt and 20% equity.
12-year structure with ~₹40 Cr potential annual revenue.
Return expectation: 9–10%.

EBITDA margins are around 19–20%.
PAT margins guided at 9–10%.

ROE at 33%+ and ROCE ~35%.

Going ahead:
• Targeting ₹500–600 Cr additional T&D orders by Mar’26 / Q1 FY27
• Increasing participation in BESS and solar hybrid tenders
• Mainboard migration possible by Mar/Apr 2026

Execution remains strong, order visibility is healthy, and the annuity layer is slowly getting built.

Still early, but the direction is clearly changing.

items.title: P/E is sometimes a flawed metric if used in isolation. It is also one of the most abused valuation ratios in markets. First, one should always look at...
items.description_html:
P/E is sometimes a flawed metric if used in isolation.

It is also one of the most abused valuation ratios in markets.

First, one should always look at normalised P/E. Reported earnings can get depressed due to exceptional losses, higher depreciation, one time provisions etc. In such cases, headline P/E does not reflect the true earning power of the business.

Second, P/E is not the right valuation metric for many sectors.

Capital intensive businesses, cyclicals, financials etc not to be valued on P/E basis.

items.title: RT Manan S. Bhatt: "Ambushed" #UntoldStoryArmy During a military training course in Mhow, India, the 1962 war with China broke out. My course was canc...
items.description_html:
RT Manan S. Bhatt
"Ambushed"
#UntoldStoryArmy

During a military training course in Mhow, India, the 1962 war with China broke out. My course was cancelled, and everyone returned to their units. However, I received special orders to stay put and await further instructions. Two days later, my posting to Jammu and Kashmir arrived – with immediate reporting required.

Having driven my car to Mhow, I had no choice but to take it back to Punjab, leave it there, and then travel on to J&K. Pressed for time, I decided to drive straight from Mhow to Delhi, a risky decision. One particularly dangerous stretch was between Gwalior and Agra, notorious for bandits and lawlessness.

Traveling alone at night through this area was foolish, but I pressed on in my trusty Hindustan-14. Around midnight, approaching a narrow pass through low hills, I found the road blocked by a fallen tree. It was an ambush. Turning back wasn't an option.

As I stopped, armed men materialized, their faces hidden. They searched me for valuables, taking my watch and wallet before moving on to my car and luggage. However, things took an unexpected turn when someone announced a "Sardar" had arrived.

Every one pulled back a little and there appeared a sturdy young man with a large mustache. As he drew near, he suddenly sprung to attention and gave me a smart salute and said ‘Sahib Ji, tussee ethay ki kar rahey ho.’ (Sir what are you doing here!)

From his voice and his face, latter day mustache notwithstanding, I recognized him. He was my tank gunner and had been discharged after his seven-year tenure of engagement. Those days soldiers were discharged after seven years and given no pension.

I responded to his query and asked him,’ Nahar Singh toon aye ki kam pharya hai (Nahar Singh what is this work you have taken on.) He said, ‘Sahib Ji admi nu kuch na kuch taan karna chaheda hai, welay beth kaay vi ki karna hai.’ (Sir one must do, some or the other work and there is no point in sitting idle!)

I told him, ‘tenu aasi eh taan nahi sakhlaya’ (we did not train you for such activity.) “Sahib ji tohati ambush laney di sikhlai kam ahh rahi hai. Sadi ambush wich koe nuks hai!.” (Sir your training in laying an ambush is proving useful. Do you find any fault with our ambush.)

He responded with a loud chuckle. He addressed his gang and said. “Dekheya sadhi regiment de officer kiney deelair hun, ekelay he rat nu is sarak tay chal rahey hun.” (do you see how brave are the officers of our regiment. They travel alone on this road at night.)

I asked him if his wife knew what work he was doing. To this he answered “Sarian gallaan wife nu thorian dassidian hun.

Oh soch di hai kay main police da kam kar rahaan hain.” (You don’t disclose everything to your wife. She thinks I am working for the police). At this one of them interjected that, “Police whi ehhoo kam kardi hai.” (Police too does this sort of work).

This raised much laughter amongst them. Then he told his men not to just stand watching but to quickly make tea for the sahib. All my stuff was put back in the car, and my watch and wallet returned. After a cup of tea and much bonhomie and handshaking I took leave of Nahar Singh and his gang.

A story by Lt Gen Harwant Singh

items.title: RT NoLimit: 🚨 SOMETHING BIG JUST HAPPENED: BlackRock just blocked investors from pulling their own money out. The world’s largest asset manager is...
items.description_html:
RT NoLimit
🚨 SOMETHING BIG JUST HAPPENED:

BlackRock just blocked investors from pulling their own money out.

The world’s largest asset manager is telling people: no, you can’t have your cash back.

This has never happened before.

BlackRock’s $26 billion private credit fund got hit with $1.2 billion in withdrawal requests this quarter.

Investors wanted 9.3% of their money back.

BlackRock said no. Capped it at 5%. Paid out $620 million and locked the rest.

That means almost HALF the people who wanted out couldn’t get out.

And it’s not just BlackRock.

Blackstone’s similar fund saw a RECORD 7.9% in redemption requests.

They had to raise their withdrawal cap and inject $400 million of their own money just to cover the demand.

Blue Owl straight up stopped honoring redemptions. Replaced them with IOUs.

BLK dropped 5%. KKR, Carlyle, Apollo, Ares, Blue Owl, and TPG all fell 5-6% with it.

The entire private credit sector sold off in a single day.

These funds lend money in illiquid loans. Loans that can’t be sold quickly.

So when too many investors want out at the same time, the fund doesn’t have the cash to pay everyone.

BlackRock also just wrote a separate $25 million loan down to ZERO.

It was valued at full price three months ago. Gone overnight.

JPMorgan’s Bill Eigen said it best: “Bad news often happens all at once. The opacity and the leverage in the sector is concerning.”

This is a $1.8 TRILLION industry.

– Rising oil.
– War in the Middle East.
– AI disrupting the software companies that borrowed heavily from these funds.
– Rate cuts off the table.

When the biggest funds in the world start telling investors you can’t have your money back…

That’s a MAJOR warning.

Btw, I’ve been an investor for more than 20 years, and when I make a new move in the market, I’ll announce it here publicly.

A lot of people will wish they followed me sooner.

items.title: RT Richard Turrin: Your ID on the Blockchain Can Fight Tinder Fakes The biggest question remains: who should issue the ID? The technology used can be ...
items.description_html:
RT Richard Turrin
Your ID on the Blockchain Can Fight Tinder Fakes

The biggest question remains: who should issue the ID? The technology used can be debated after determining who controls the ID.

Should it be a form of state-issued Digital Public Infrastructure, as with India's Aadhaar, or should it pass to Web3 companies who believe in identity kept out of reach of the state?

While the debate between the state and Web3 remains lively, what most agree on is that this isn't a job for Google or Apple as much as they would like to have it.

Note that in the US, IRS electronic filing depends on "ID .me" a big tech solution paving the way for Google and Apple to take over.

Perhaps the best solution yet is the EU's EUDI wallet, which, while state-backed, supports decentralized identity (DID) standards, allowing users to own and manage their identity data rather than relying on central authorities like Google or Facebook.

While Web3 wants to be free of the state, it's hard to see such a DID system interacting with state-sponsored tax and social benefits.

Kudos to @andymukherjee70

items.title: Does ethanol blending actually save fossil fuel consumption. India is usually compared to Brazil Sharing the math below and answer is - Not Really Key...
items.description_html:
Does ethanol blending actually save fossil fuel consumption. India is usually compared to Brazil

Sharing the math below and answer is - Not Really

Key Take Away - It makes way more sense in Brazil as their sugar cane is rain fed, while in India we use quite a bit of irrigation

India saves ~27%. Brazil saves ~87%. The difference is almost entirely irrigation — Indian sugarcane is pumped with majorly coal-fired electricity, Brazilian cane is rainfed. India's ethanol program mostly swaps imported crude for domestic coal and gas. Brazil's genuinely displaces fossil fuels.

Write off peaked in FY20 at Rs 1.59 tn. It has dropped to Rs 47,568 crore in FY25. Banks have written off Rs 31,723 crore in FY15, Rs 40,416 crore in FY16, Rs 68,308 crore in FY 17, Rs 99,132 crore in FY18 & Rs1.59 tn in FY19, minister said in a written reply to the Lok Sabha.

items.title: Two quick takeaways from the platform fee hike by Zomato and Swiggy: - Food delivery continues to be the cash cow, helping offset losses from quick co...
items.description_html:
Two quick takeaways from the platform fee hike by Zomato and Swiggy:

- Food delivery continues to be the cash cow, helping offset losses from quick commerce, especially for Swiggy, which needs to get to breakeven soon. This is also a key advantage that both companies have over Zepto.

- For now, Zomato and Swiggy are not seeing a significant impact on their market share from newer platforms like Rapido’s Ownly and Swish. It will be interesting to see whether these recently well-funded players can leverage this.


Madhav Chanchani: Both Zomato and Swiggy have increased platform fees to Rs 17.58, a jump of Rs 2.59 per order.

Collectively, this will add over Rs 100 crore directly to their bottom line per quarter.

About Rs 60-65 crore for Zomato and Rs 40–45 crore for Swiggy, based on their order volumes.

items.title: Looks like the new owners will keep the Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB) brand, or at least keep the option open. They will enter into a deal with Un...
items.description_html:
Looks like the new owners will keep the Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB) brand, or at least keep the option open.

They will enter into a deal with United Spirits to use the brand after the transaction closes.

The RCB team is named after United Spirits’ Royal Challenge whisky brand.

The new owners - Blitzers, Blackstone, Aditya Birla Group, and the Times of India Group - are buying RCB for $1.78 billion from United Spirits.

The deal is expected to close in six months.

items.description_html:
RT Variety
Disney has ended its partnership with OpenAI after Sora is discontinued. The company had plans to take a $1 billion stake in the AI company.

A Disney rep said in a statement to Variety: “As the nascent AI field advances rapidly, we respect OpenAI’s decision to exit the video generation business and to shift its priorities elsewhere. We appreciate the constructive collaboration between our teams and what we learned from it, and we will continue to engage with AI platforms to find new ways to meet fans where they are while responsibly embracing new technologies that respect IP and the rights of creators.”

items.description_html:
For context, Chinese firms such as Alibaba Group and Tencent were among the biggest growth-stage backers of internet companies such as Zomato, Paytm, and Swiggy before 2020.

Chinese companies and venture firms were investors in 18 of the 30 Unicorns India had back then. They had invested $4-5 billion in Indian startups between 2017-2020.

It will be interesting to see whether strategics make a comeback or if the capital will mostly come through venture capital funds.


Madhav Chanchani: It’s official: Chinese capital can now return to India’s startup ecosystem.

The government has relaxed the Press Note 3 restrictions implemented in 2020.

Investments of up to a 10% stake will now be allowed under the automatic route.
